chunks.text: Information for Current Students - EECS at Berkeley. Information for Current Students. Know your rights! It's important to know your rights if you're ever approached by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Stay informed and prepared—visit our resources page for more information.
chunks.question: What should EECS students know about their rights?
chunks.answer: Know rights when approached by ICE.

chunks.text: Graduate Students: Information for Graduate Students, New Graduate Student Guide. Department Resources: Student Computing and Email, Facilities, Safety & Space (Cory & Soda Hall building safety, space & maintenance, and department vehicle information), Administrative Services (Lost and Found, mail, and shipping/receiving. For faculty office hours, see their EECS homepage), Center for Student Affairs (Individual academic and administrative assistance for all EECS students), EECS Commencement.
chunks.question: What department resources are available for EECS students?
chunks.answer: Computing, facilities, admin services, student affairs.

chunks.text: Undergraduate Students: Advice and support, degree requirements, sample study plans, declaring and changing majors and minors, commencement, research opportunities, special programs (honors, study abroad, etc.). Information for Undergraduates, EECS Major, CS Major, ECE Major, Undergraduate Research, EECS Study Abroad Program.
chunks.question: What support is offered to undergraduate EECS students?
chunks.answer: Advice, degree requirements, research, special programs.

chunks.text: General: Course Schedules and Information, Curricular Practical Training (CPT), In Times of Stress, Department Policy on Academic Dishonesty, Student Concerns and Grievances (Contacts for grievances and sexual harassment), Student Organizations, Student Awards (Winners, descriptions, and nominations), Resources for Underrepresented Students.
chunks.question: What general resources does EECS provide?
chunks.answer: Course info, CPT, policies, grievances, awards.

chunks.text: ASEs (Academic Student Employees): Information for ASEs (TAs, UCS2s, Readers, Tutors, & UCS1s), Prospective ASEs (Applications, deadlines, selection process), Current ASEs (Time sheets, payroll FAQs, financial information, course resources), Resources for TAs and UCS2s (Resource guide for course staff to answer questions and help navigate responsibilities).
chunks.question: What information is available for Academic Student Employees?
chunks.answer: Prospective and current ASE resources and guides.

chunks.text: Find a Job: Internships, job postings, competitions, and recruiting events for current EECS and CS students. Handshake, Open Faculty Positions.
chunks.question: How can EECS students find jobs?
chunks.answer: Internships, job postings, events via Handshake.
